paper_title: Elemental Analysis of Single Cells and Sampling Procedures

methods.description: Process for collecting, preparing, and analyzing the elemental composition of single microphytoplankton cells using X-ray Microanalysis (XRMA) with a Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM). This method involves sample collection, concentration, mounting on grids, SEM imaging, XRMA data acquisition, and initial data processing including cell measurement, volume calculation, calibration, and filtering.

steps.description: Water samples for XRMA analysis were collected in 5 to 8 L plastic bottles after pre-screening with a 200 μm nylon mesh to remove large mesozooplankton.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect water samples and pre-screen with mesh

steps.description: Briefly, the cells were concentrated onto a 20 μm nylon mesh and washed to remove the salt.
steps.searchable_summary: Concentrate and wash cells using mesh

steps.description: After washing, the cells were centrifuged at 15°C over a 10 mm diameter Cu grid coated with formvar.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge cells onto a coated grid

steps.description: The grid was air-dried and stored in a vacuum chamber until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Air-dry and store grid in vacuum

steps.description: Cells were viewed and analysed for elements in a Hitachi S-3500N Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM), equipped with an energy-dispersive spectrometer Si(Li) detector (Bruker AXS).
steps.searchable_summary: Set up SEM and EDX detector for analysis

steps.description: To avoid interference of holder material with sample elements, as well as with the X-ray spectra, the sample grid was mounted over a customized high-resolution holder (Hitachi) at a distance of 17 mm over the holder bottom [15].
steps.searchable_summary: Mount grid on specialized SEM holder

steps.description: The microscope was operated at 15 kV of electron beam energy, 15 mm of working distance, accumulation time of 100 sec (live time) per analysis, and 15°of tilt angle.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure SEM operating parameters for XRMA
inputs: operating parameters (15 kV, 15 mm WD, 100 sec live time, 15° tilt)

steps.description: The X-ray spectrum was recorded from an area that circumscribes the specimen [14].
steps.searchable_summary: Record X-ray spectrum from specimen area

steps.description: The software used to acquire the spectra and to obtain the counts per second of each element present in the spectra was QUANTAX 1.6 (Bruker AXS).
steps.searchable_summary: Process X-ray spectra using QUANTAX to get elemental counts

steps.description: Cells were identified by the imaging system...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify cells from SEM images

steps.description: ...and two perpendicular axes of the cell were measured from the SEM pictures of the analysed cells using the software QUARTZ PCI 5.1.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure cell dimensions from SEM images using QUARTZ PCI

steps.description: After, cell volumes were calculated following Sun and Liu [29], assuming depth to be half the width, except in Protoperidinium sp. and Tripos sp., for which depth was assumed to be equal to width.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate cell volumes based on dimensions and genus-specific assumptions

steps.description: Latex beads (Agar Scientific) were used to calibrate carbon...
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate carbon quantification using latex beads

steps.description: ...and the calibration constants for other elements were obtained following Norland et al. [14].
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain calibration constants for other elements using referenced method

steps.description: In the present study, only elemental quotas above the detection limit, which are more than 98% of the quotas measured, are shown and used to calculate ratios. The detection limit of the method is 0.16% [15].
steps.searchable_summary: Filter elemental quotas based on detection limit

steps.description: Total cellular dry weight per cell was calculated adding the molecular weight of all the quantified elements (C, N, O, Na, Mg, Al, Si, P, S, Cl, K and Ca), and assuming 1.6 mols of H per each mol of C (from equation 2 in Fraga [30]).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate total cellular dry weight from elemental composition
inputs: filtered elemental quotas (C, N, O, Na, Mg, Al, Si, P, S, Cl, K, Ca)
outputs: total cellular dry weight per cell

methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Single Cell Elemental Data
methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the elemental composition and volume data of single microphytoplankton cells, including regression analysis, ratio estimation, outlier detection, and group comparisons.

steps.description: Element vs. volume regressions were studied first normalizing the variables by log transformation.
steps.searchable_summary: Log transform variables for regression analysis

steps.description: Conversion factors to predict cellular carbon from cell volumes were estimated as the slope of the regression between element mass and volume, using Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) regression model type I [31,32].
steps.searchable_summary: Perform OLS regression (Type I) to predict carbon from volume

steps.description: Elemental ratios can be estimated as the average of individual ratios in a given population, or as the slope of the linear regression of each element with P (e.g. [33,34,35]). The linear regression method minimizes the error between ratios when the concentrations are low [36], and so it is used in this study to compare ratios between phylogenetic groups.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate elemental ratios using linear regression with Phosphorus
inputs: elemental quotas (e.g., C, N, P, etc.)

steps.description: Element vs. element regressions were estimated using Standard Major Axis (SMA), a type II regression model that takes into account differences in the scale of both axes (e.g. C:P relationship).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform SMA regression (Type II) for element vs. element relationships
inputs: elemental quotas (pairs, e.g., C and P)

steps.description: Extreme values were identified using the Quartile method (values smaller/higher than the first/third quartile minus/plus three times the interquartile range)...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify outliers using Quartile method

steps.description: ...and not used to calculate regression lines.
steps.searchable_summary: Exclude outliers from regression analysis

steps.description: The stoichiometries were first calculated independently for each group.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate stoichiometries per phylogenetic group
inputs: elemental quota data (grouped by phylogenetic group)

steps.description: Afterwards, tests for differences between y-intercepts and slopes of diatoms and dinoflagellates were performed.
steps.searchable_summary: Test for differences in regression parameters between diatoms and dinoflagellates
inputs: regression parameters (slopes, intercepts) for diatoms and dinoflagellates

steps.description: The software SMATR v2.0 [37] was used to calculate both OLS and SMA slopes and intercepts, along with p-values and confidence intervals, as well as differences and shifts in slopes between dinoflagellates and diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate regression parameters and group differences using SMATR v2.0

steps.description: Inter-and intra-generic differences between elemental concentrations and stoichiometry were studied with the software PAST v3.06 [38].
steps.searchable_summary: Study inter/intra-generic differences using PAST v3.06

steps.description: Univariate non-parametric tests Mann-Whitney U was used in case of 2 groups, and Kruskal-Wallis for 3 groups.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform non-parametric tests (Mann-Whitney U, Kruskal-Wallis)

steps.description: If differences were found, a Mann-Whitney pairwise comparison with Bonferroni corrected p-values was performed.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform pairwise Mann-Whitney test with Bonferroni correction
inputs: results from Kruskal-Wallis test indicating differences

steps.description: Statistical significance was accepted when p 0.01.
steps.searchable_summary: Set statistical significance level threshold

methods.description: Procedure for collecting surface water samples at specific locations along the Catalan coast with different environmental characteristics for subsequent analysis. This includes selecting sites, collecting samples, and measuring basic biogeochemical parameters (methods for parameter measurement are referenced externally).

steps.description: Four surface water samples were taken at three different locations along the Catalan coast ( Fig 1) with different environmental characteristics ( Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Select and document sampling locations and characteristics

steps.description: The first sample was taken inside the harbour of Arenys de Mar... The second sample was taken at the Blanes Bay Microbial Observatory... The remaining two samples were both taken... at the Coastal Monitoring Station of Barcelona...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect surface water samples at specified locations and times using plastic bottles
inputs: sampling dates (Harbour: end of bloom; Bay: end of spring bloom; CS Barcelona: Jan 2006, May 2006)

steps.description: Temperature, salinity, and other basic parameters such as chlorophyll a (Chla) concentration and dissolved inorganic nutrients (NO 3 -, NO 2 -, NH 4 + , PO 4 3-and Si[OH] 4 ) were measured at all sites (Table 1). A full description of the methods used to analyse those parameters at the Harbour, Bay, and Continental Shelf sites are given in Alonso-Sáez et al. [24], Van Lenning et al. [27] and Romero et al. [28], and Arin et al. [26], respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure basic biogeochemical parameters (methods referenced externally)
